sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: RC Lens, sitting at 8th place with 48 points, host Auxerre, who are positioned 10th with 41 points, in Round 31 at the iconic Stade Bollaert-Delelis in Lens. This matchup promises intrigue as both teams are closely matched in the league standings. The referee for the game, Mathieu Vernice, is known for averaging 4.1 yellow cards and 0.31 red cards per game, which could influence the tempo and discipline on the field. With both teams hungry for points to climb the table, expect a competitive clash in this Ligue 1 encounter.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between RC Lens and Auxerre, Lens have a slight edge with 2 wins and 2 draws, while Auxerre have yet to secure a victory. Their most recent encounter on December 14, 2024, ended in an exciting 2-2 draw, with RC Lens’ Przemysław Frankowski earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.1 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends show Lens have not lost to Auxerre in their last 7 meetings and have been first to score in 6 of those 7 games, indicating a psychological advantage. Additionally, matches between these two often see less than 10.5 corners, with this trend continuing in 5 of their last 6 encounters.

sections.subtitle: RC Lens Team Form and Key Players
sections.text: RC Lens have scored 37 goals this season, ranking 12th in the league, but they lead the division in goals versus expected goals difference with a remarkable 14.0021 overperformance, ranked 1st. Their defensive solidity is evident with 12 clean sheets, also a league-best, and only 38 goals conceded, placing them 4th in least goals conceded. Standout player Neil El Aynaoui, a midfielder, is in fine form over the last 5 matches with a 7.62 average Sofascore rating, 2 goals, and 1 assist. Another key figure is Adrien Thomasson, who has contributed 2 goals and 6 assists, boasting a 7.09 rating. Defensively, Malang Sarr shines with a league rank of 3 in aerial duels won at 76%, which could be crucial against Auxerre’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Auxerre Team Form and Key Players
sections.text: Auxerre have shown attacking flair with players like Gaëtan Perrin leading the charge; he ranks 1st in the league for assists with 10, alongside 9 goals and a 7.34 average rating, while also ranking 2nd in key passes (72) and big chances created (18). Defender Jubal adds to their threat with 6 goals and a 7.26 rating, showing their ability to score from set pieces. Lassine Sinayoko has chipped in with 4 goals and 7 assists, maintaining a solid 6.95 rating. However, Auxerre struggle defensively, as seen in their head-to-head trend of failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 games against Lens. Missing key midfielder Hamed Junior Traorè, with 10 goals this season, could further impact their balance.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights for RC Lens
sections.text: RC Lens excel in several statistical categories, ranking 1st in accurate crosses with 175 and clean sheets with 12, while also placing 2nd in corners per game at 5.625 and penalty area entries per game at 32.03125. Their pressing game is strong, ranking 4th in possessions won in the final third per game at 4.78125. However, they rank poorly in converting big chances, sitting 17th with a conversion rate of 32.94%, having missed 57 big chances. Goal distribution shows Lens are most dangerous late in games, scoring 28.95% of their goals between the 76th and 90th minutes. On the disciplinary front, they’ve committed the most fouls in the league with 473 and rank 17th in least yellow cards with 78, averaging 2.4375 per game.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights for Auxerre
sections.text: Auxerre’s goal distribution indicates they peak in the latter stages of matches, with 25.53% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes and another 19.15% between the 61st and 75th minutes. They also score consistently just before halftime, netting 19.15% of goals between the 31st and 45th minutes. Midfielder Assane Dioussé stands out with a league rank of 5 for long balls as a percentage of total passes at 9.1%, showcasing their direct style. However, recent team streaks reveal a tendency for low-scoring games, with less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 matches. Their disciplinary record aligns with Lens, often seeing less than 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 games.

sections.subtitle: Key Matchups and Missing Players
sections.text: The battle in midfield could be decisive, with Lens’ Neil El Aynaoui facing off against Auxerre’s Gaëtan Perrin, whose league-leading 10 assists and 9 goals make him a constant threat. On the defensive end, Lens’ Malang Sarr, with his aerial dominance, will look to neutralize Auxerre’s Jubal, who has scored 6 goals from the back. Lens are missing several players, including forward Florian Sotoca and midfielder Deiver Machado, which could dent their attacking depth. Auxerre, without Hamed Junior Traorè and Elisha Owusu, may struggle to maintain midfield control. These absences could tilt the balance slightly in favor of the home side, who have more depth to cover.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Expectations
sections.text: RC Lens are likely to leverage their league-leading accurate crosses (175) and high ranking in corners per game (5.625, 2nd) to create scoring opportunities, especially given their home advantage at Stade Bollaert-Delelis. Their strong pressing game, ranked 4th in possessions won in the final third, could disrupt Auxerre’s buildup play. Auxerre, on the other hand, will rely on Gaëtan Perrin’s creativity, with his 18 big chances created (ranked 2nd), to exploit any defensive lapses from Lens. Both teams’ tendency for low-scoring games, as seen in streaks of less than 2.5 goals in recent matches, suggests a tight affair. Disciplinary trends, with both sides often seeing less than 4.5 cards in recent games, might mean a relatively clean contest despite Lens’ league-high 473 fouls.
